During trouble shooting by FTI,it has been observed that all 4 MPCB of SAF1 &SAF2 (QM05,QM06,QM07 & QM08) are in tripped condition in VAC control panel.In TCMS VDU follwing fault werw logged.
a) VAC-1 fault- Supply Air Fan -1 CB trip.
b) VAC-1 fault - Supply fan -2 CB trip.
c) VAC Major Fault
d) VAC 1 (CN1) transmission error.
e) VAC 1 (CN2) transmission error.
f) VAC 1 (CN1) - No response time out.

Further ,VAC was triggered to run in service mode and found that VAC is not running in EVM mode.Inverter has been checked at all pins by using multimeter & found as below.
Pin No. 1 &2 110 V DC - found ok (112V)
Pin No. 3 EMI ok signal - found ok (112 V)
Pin No. 4 EMI ok signal - found not ok (2-3 V)
Pin No. 5 & 6 (110V dc) start + & start - found ok (112 V)
Pin No. 7,8,&9 (U,V & W) found not ok.(0 V)
Based on above,emergency inverter fault has been assured.
Faulty Sl No.- 0439 ( modified one)
Healthy Sl No.- 235 ( modified one)

From above,it is very much clear that even modified emergency inverter is not stable and getting failed within a duration of 2 months.Our end customer is very much annoyed with such  type of unstable faults & told to stop further execution of EMI FMI till OEM investigation report submission.

M/s FTI is requested to submit detailed investigation report for the faulty modified emergency inverter at the earliest to proceed with further inverter FMI.

Metro Railway reported that in TS#04-MC2-VAC1 condenser fan 2 CB was tripped at IBL. During investigation, VAC control panel was checked and found that QM04 MPCB of CDF2 in tripped condition. CDF2 wiring was checked for any loose connection, cable cut, damage, cable for any grounding, earthing, shorting & found all normal. 

CDF2 current was measured and found as:
CDF2 U stable current- 1.5A
CDF2 V stable current- 1.5A
CDF2 W stable current- 1.6A

Winding resistance of CDF2 was measured:
CDF2 U vs V- 20.7 Ohm
CDF2 U vs W- 20.7 Ohm
CDF2 V vs W- 20.7 Ohm

Meggering of CDF2 was done and found ok across phase v/s earth.

CDF motor was run for 4 to 5 minutes, in that while running it 5th time manually the CDF2 was found with abnormal sound. After that CDF2 motor got stuck and stopped working. Suddenly, QM04 CB was tripped. So FTI Local Team has confirmed that CDF motor faulty. CDF motor was replaced from DLP stock. 

After that VAC was run for 30 minutes and no abnormality was found. Functional test done and found ok.

On 03.08.22 in depot. During trouble shooting following checking has been done.
A) SAF-1, 2 pole overload quick connector  male & female pins has been checked & found ok.
B) Continuity between X05 harting,pin no 32,33 & overload quick connector and found ok.
C) Continuity between SAF-1 ,2 pole overload quick connector & PLC connector P2 ,b7 found ok.

Even after that.SAF 1 oveload feedback fault is persisting.
So, on supect basic, PLC has been replaced from DLP stock.VAC function has been checked & found SAF overoad feedback fault is not persisting but PLC is not communicating with TCMS.It is showing unknown in TCMS.In reffered PLC ,SW was once again flashed & controller & train has been restarted but issue did not resolve.
So,PLC was again replaced with other PLC from DLP stock 2nd time and VAC function hasbeen checkd & found all ok.

TS # 5, TC – 2, VAC – 2, Circuit – 2, LP fault was reported in main line on 23.08.22 by Metro Railway. During investigation on 24.08.22, it was found that fault was not active. While checking found that refrigerant pressure is comparatively low in circuit – 2. LP is maintaining around 1.7 to 1.8 bar while running VAC in auto mode. So, it was suspected that there is a leakage in circuit – 2.
Referred VAC was brought to floor for performing leakage test & further activity. While checking minor leakage found at Schrader Valve of HP-2 Sensor of circuit-2 (thread area). The same had been replaced along with Sight Glass and Filter/Drier from FTI stock by de-brazing & brazing activity. After topping up with refrigerant, subject VAC was put back to train. During testing at train level, Compressor – 2 MPCB (QM – 02) tripped twice.
On 29.08.22, meggering of compressor done at 500 V & 1000 V and resistance value found as 550 MΩ & 700 MΩ in each phase respectively. Winding resistance also checked & found as below:
U-V – 0.9 Ω
V-W – 0.9 Ω
W-U – 1 Ω
Current drawn when compressor start running in all 3 phase are as below:
On 27th Aug’22
U = 0 A
V = 21 A
W = 21 A
Current Value of V and W phase were continuously increasing.
On 29th Aug’22
U = 14.1 A
V = 15.5 A
W = 15.1 A
On 29th Aug’22, after running of VAC in auto mode for 1.5 hrs. again compressor CB (QM02) tripped.
Based on above, compressor fault has been assured.
Faulty Sl No. –   18CC2362Z
Healthy Sl No. – 21HH2569Z
After replacement with  healthy compressor from DLP stock, fault is not persisting.

Note: Schrader Valve is replaced from FTI stock & Compressor is replaced from DLP stock.
VAC in SI. no.: C19-4170 (GA)
VAC out SI. No.: F18-4056

column6:
TS # 5, TC – 2, VAC – 2, Circuit – 2, LP fault was reported in main line on 23.08.22 by Metro Railway. During investigation on 24.08.22, it was found that fault was not active. While checking found that refrigerant pressure is comparatively low in circuit – 2. LP is maintaining around 1.7 to 1.8 bar while VAC is running in auto mode. So, it was suspected that there is a leakage in circuit – 2.
Referred VAC was brought to floor for performing leakage test & further activity. While checking minor leakage found at Schrader Valve of HP-2 Sensor of circuit-2 (thread area). The same had been replaced along with Sight Glass and Filter/Drier from FTI stock by de-brazing & brazing activity. After topping up with refrigerant, subject VAC was put back to train. During testing at train level, Compressor – 2 MPCB (QM – 02) tripped thrice.
Based on above, compressor fault has been assured & compressor has been replaced with healthy one from DLP stock. After replacement of compressor, during nitrogen leak test, leakage found in HP-2 vibration damper flexible hose (discharge side of circuit-2). After receipt of stock, referred item has been replacement by doing de-brazing/brazing activity.
Vibration Damper HP-2 S. No. Out: E3433-06
Vibration Damper HP-2 S. No. In:   E3051--51
 While doing nitrogen leakage test at 18 bar again leakage found in LP-2 vibration damper flexible hose (suction side of circuit-2). After receipt, same has been fitted to VAC by doing de-brazing/brazing activity. After that nitrogen flushing done & nitrogen leakage test performed at 16 bar pressure. No leakage found.
Vibration Damper LP-2 S. No. Out: E3389-21
Vibration Damper LP-2 S. No. In: E3992-07
Vacuuming process done and then 7.5 kg R134A refrigerant charged. When VAC was made to run at shop floor during testing again leakage found in LP – 1, Vibration Damper. (suction side of circuit 1). On receipt from FTI, Hosur, referred item has been replaced.
Vibration Damper LP-1 S. No. Out: E3389-41
Vibration Damper LP-1 S. No. In: E3992-19
As a precautionary measure, HP-1 vibration damper has also been replaced by FTI.
Vibration Damper HP-1 S. No. Out: E3422-17
Vibration Damper HP-1 S. No. In: E3971-60
Vacuuming done in circuit – 1, 7.5 kg refrigerant R134A charged in circuit – 1. Refrigerant leakage test done with electronic leak detector. Complete assembly of VAC done and shop floor testing done. VAC found fit for service.
VAC Tested at shop floor once again, VAC run for 2 hours and found OK. No abnormality found.

Metro Railway reported that DBV was  not working properly in DMC-1 side during train fitness at IBL. We checked above problem jointly with K.B.I.
TS#09 DMC1-DBV lever operated in discharging mode from the charging mode & brake pipe pressure was not getting to zero bar(pressure reduced up to 1bar only)in dial gauge.
When DBV lever operated in Lap mode from discharge mode then brake pipe pressure increased from 1 bar to 5 bar.BP pressure should be stable in LAP mode.
BPIMV wiring checked from our side ( BEML) and found 110 V was coming in DMC-2. BPIMV coil resistance checked & found OL in DMC-2.
So K.B.I has confirmed that BPIMV coil is faulty in DMC-2 side.

TS# 06, MC2, VAC 1, Circuit 2, LP fault was reported in main line on 18.11.22 by Metro Railway. During investigation on 19.11.22, it was found that fault was active. While checking found that LP is going down to 1.5 bar within 1 minute and refrigerant pressure is comparatively low in circuit – 2. LP is maintaining around 1.3 to 1.5 bar while VAC is running in auto mode. So, it was suspected that there is a leakage in circuit – 2.
Referred VAC was brought to floor for performing leakage test & further activity. While checking referred VAC was charged with 1.7 kg RS134A gas in circuit 2, but no improvement has been observed in LP-2. So it was concluded that there is no leakage in the VAC.
Further, it has been observed that group - 2, expansion valve - 2 input side (HP side) was colder in comparison to other expansion valve and it is hotter at LP side in comparison to other TXVs. 
Based on that it was concluded that either expansion valve is malfunctioning or filter.  On suspect basis, it was decided to replace expansion valve 2 of Group-2 and Filter drier and sight glass for Group-2 from DLP stock.
Expansion valve Out s.no: BE1018A 
Expansion valve In s.no:    BE4018G
Vacuuming done for circuit – 2, 7.5 kg refrigerant R134A charged in circuit – 2. Refrigerant leakage test done with electronic leak detector. Complete assembly of VAC done and shop floor testing done. VAC found fit for service.
VAC Tested at shop floor once again, VAC run for 2 hours and found OK. No abnormality found.  
VAC in SI. No.: C18-4019
VAC out SI. No.: G18-4061.

Incident:
On 15.04.2023 after powering On rake MR 601 for offering rake for revenue service in the morning, it was noticed that MR pressure was not building up (-2 bar) in car DMC 1 (1101) of the rake, whereas BP and BC pressures were showing normal values (5 bar & 2.4 bar) in pressure gauge. Parking brake pressure valves were also low (2.2 Bar). Even after Aux reset the problem was not reset, and consequently, the rake could not be offered for revenue service. The same problem of MR pressure not building up was again reported just before shunting of rake from IBL to SBL on 21.04.2023. On all this occasions it was found that after MR ball cock (isolation cock) was operated from normal to isolation, and then back to normal mode in DM1 & TC1 cars, MR pressure started building up in DM1 car of MR 601.
Action taken at Depot:
This issue was checked jointly with FTI and Metro Railways in DM1 car of rake MR601 on 26.04.2023 during inspection of MR601. it was found that when MR pressure is almost drained of in the rake, and rake is powered on with 750 V, MR air flow across the hose between TC1 and DMC1 is getting obstructed and can not reach DM 1 car, whereas there is no leakage any where in the pipe line as well as MR hose. TCMS data also showed that R- Pressure value was about 2 bar in DM car , it was 9.6 bar in the other car.
So the single hose pressure protection valves (Model/part no: AB839-T8) in DMC 1 and the adjacent side of TC1 car were lowered from coach with support your FTI location team. Then visual inspection and checking of air at high pressure through the inlets were carried out in the side valves but nothing abnormal was noticed in either of them. So after cleaning, the two Single hose pressure protection valves were fitted back to their respective coaches, in their previous condition. further , the rake has been tested in test track following completion of B8 schedule on 27.04.2023, wherein no problem was found. More over , since there is no spare unit of single hose pressure protection valves (Part no : AB839-T8) available in GA Spare, these valves could not be replaced in MR601. Non stop demand has been made of procurement of 06 (Six) Nos. of this item by Metro Railway.
Reason for the failure :
The cause of the failure of MR pressure not building up in DM1 coach of MR601 rake could not be directly estabished. it is suspected that the either of these two single Hose pressure protection valves (Model/Part no : AB8939-T8) - in DMC 1 (SI. No : H17 043, make FAIVELEY), or in the adjustment - to - DMC1 side of TC 1 car (SI. No : H17 036, make FAIVELEY) may be getting stuck up during pressure build up after a period of zero air (MR) pressure, which is most likely causing the problem of MR pressure not building up in DMC 1 car. However, through visual inspection alone it is not posible to ascertain precisely which of the two valves is having any problem. more over, ther is no test jig available at depot for checking of the valve. 
In OEM literature for Single Hose Pressure Protection Valve (Maintenance manual) there is no mention of some important parameters viz. Valves pressure differential setting, reaction time, residual MR pressure after the valve actuates, life time, ect.
column26: Issue is under monioring.

On dated 28.05.2024, Low pressure fault in system- 1 has been reported in mainline in TS#14-MC2 -VAC1, system-1.
During investigation by FTI at depot on 29.05.2024, fault was found active. LP1 pressure is going down to 1.5 bar within 2 minutes. & HP1 pressure values were observed low. LP1 pressure was maintaining around 1.5 to 1.7 bar pressure while running VAC in auto mode. So, leakage in the circuit had been confirmed. To find the exact location of leakage, VAC Sl No. N18 4135 was brought to floor for further investigation. During N2 leakage test, it has been found that, leakage is from circuit-1 condenser coil output pipe.
To make train fit immediately for revenue service, faulty VAC has been swapped with healthy VAC. The details of the same is as under:
 VAC IN Sl No. - H18 4110
 VAC OUT Sl No. - N18 4135
Afterwards during investigation at shop floor, dent mark observed on the inner side of condenser coil & also refrigerant spread was observed on the surface of the coil. Further, gap between inner side of condenser coil & discharge flexible hose has been checked. It has been found that gap was very less (below 5 mm). 
After mutual discussion between FTI, BEML & customer (MR), the faulty condenser coil copper tube has been brazed properly for arresting the gas leakage by following proper procedure. While fitment of repaired condenser coil, OEM has confirmed that they have maintained a gap of 11 mm between the condenser and discharge Flexible hose. 
Repaired Condenser coil Sl no. 135 
Correction/ corrective action result:
1.Investigation report to be submitted for closure of the NCR.
2. M/s Faiveley to take care of minimum gap of 5-11 mm between condenser coil & flexible damper.  
Note: – This is 2nd case of condense coil leakage due to no gap between condenser coil & flexible damper. 1st one was TS#10, MC2, VAC1, system-1 dt 04.11.2022.

TS# 05, DMC2 Cab VAC, LP fault is reported in depot on 01.10.2024 by Metro Railway. During investigation on 02.10.2024, it is found that fault is active. While checking from MONA it is observed that LP switch current rating is getting decreased to -54.70 within few second of running. Further, with the help of electronic leak detector, system has been checked for any leakage in the circuit. But no leakage has been found. In addition to this health of all component of the cab vac has been checked & found ok. So, it was suspected that may be expansion valve would have malfunctioned or there may be chocking at filter.
Based on that expansion valve, air drier filter & sight glass have been replaced with the healthy one from DLP stock. Further cab VAC is made to run for 1+1½ hr. No abnormality observed. The referred VAC has been installed back to train.
Within a week's time, the same fault repeated. This time on checking it is found that refrigerant leakage is there from the LP switch injection valve. It has been observed that thread of the injection valve of LP switch is damaged.
Referred cab vac has been brought to floor for replacement of injection valve by de-brazing & brazing activity.
Cab vac tested at shop floor once again, VAC run for 2 hours and found OK. No abnormality found. VAC is installed back to train side.
Expansion valve Out s.no: N0818A
Expansion valve In s.no: N3818F.
Correction/ corrective action result: 
1. Item to be repaired/replaced at the earliest. 
2. Investigation report to be submitted for closure of the NCR.

TS#14-TC1- Compressor -1- First time Main Compressor long time running fault observed on 25.06.2024 during revenue operation.
We have checked the compressor wiring, air leakage in train line & compressor operation at Depot and found it healthy on 26.06.2024. Then the train was sent to the test track to the check compressor functionality and the Main Compressor long time running fault didn't report.
Next time Train set#14 was sent to revenue operation and Second time Main Compressor long time running fault was observed on 05.07.2024 during revenue operation.
We have jointly checked with KBI TC1- Compressor -1 pressure build up time from 5.8
bar to 8 bar in 2.18 minutes after switching off the remaining 3 nos compressors CB then
the same things simulated in TC2-Compressor-1 and it takes 2.16 minutes at Depot Kolkata
So it's working normally at depot but fault is occurring in revenue operation
We have checked the TCMS Event log and RTDM data and observed the TC1-Compressor -1 for a long time running during the revenue operation. That's why the above fault is coming. TC1- Compressor-1 is not able to produce the required air pressure during revenue
operation as per TCMS data. KBI Field Officer has confirmed that Compressor is faulty after above investigation.
Correction/ corrective action result:  
1.M/s KBI has be replaced air compressor on urgent basis because TS#14 is not
going to revenue operation since August 2024 due to compressor issue and Train kept on BEML/KBI account. Customer can be extended the DLP of TS#14 due to above issue.
2. Please submit FIR and replace the compressor for NCR Closer.

RCCB trip and VAC Major Fault reported in mainline on 12.11.2024. In depot, it is found that the particular VAC is running in emergency mode even though 415 V is available for train. During the commissioning of TS15, it was observed that the TFTs at the specified location consistently displayed a red status on the TGUT. Upon investigation, it was determined that power light was not glowing on the same TFT.
Testing Procedure: To further assess the situation, we installed another TFT at the same location for testing purposes. This replacement TFT functioned normally, indicating that the issue lies with the original TFTs and not with the power supply or installation location.
Conclusion: The original TFTs are deemed faulty due to the lack of power and their inability to operate correctly. A non-conformity report is hereby generated to address this issue and initiate corrective actions.
Correction/ corrective action result: 
Conduct a thorough analysis of the faulty TFTs to determine the root cause of the power failure. This may involve reviewing the manufacturing process, electrical connections, and any potential design flaws. Initiate the process for replacing the faulty TFTs with new units. Ensure that the replacement TFTs meet all quality and performance standards as per TELEVIC's specifications.

Metro Railway has reported that TC2, VAC2 is running in emergency mode in mainline even though 415 V DC supply is available. While investigating at depot it has been observed that VAC Major fault is active for referred car on TCMS VDU.
When checked on Mona PTU, it is found that RCCB trip & Compressor -1 CB trip faults are active in event log. Further when physically checked in VAC control panel, RCCB (QF01) & Compressor-1 MPCB (QM01) are found in tripped condition
During investigation, both compressors winding resistance across all the phases are checked & found balanced i.e., 1.2 Ω, 1.3 Ω & 1.3 Ω.  Further all related wirings are checked for any looseness, cut mark or damage, found everything normal. 
Further, both compressors winding is checked with respect to ground. It is found that for compressor 1 winding (U, V & W) Vs ground is showing value measured as 50 KΩ which should actually should show as open loop. 
Based on above, compressor 1 fault has been assured.
Faulty Sl No. –   18DK2990Z
Healthy Sl No. – 23HE1914Z
After replacement with healthy compressor from DLP stock, fault is not persisting.
Correction/ corrective action result: 
1. Compressor is replaced from DLP stock.
2. Item to be repaired/ replaced in DLP at the earliest.
3. Investigation report to be submitted for closure of the NCR.
Note: – This is 4th compressor failure after flashing of SW 0.12.

To assure about whether smoke sensor is faulty or sensor is misbehaving, referred smoke sensor has been cleaned & reinstalled to VAC. But fault is still persisting. Based on same Smoke sensor is declared faulty by FTI. After replacement function test done & found ok.
Healthy (In) item Sl. No:   C1517082018
Faulty (Out) item Sl. No:  CH1516121001
Thereafter On-board test was done and found normal.
Correction/ corrective action result: 
Smoke sensor has been replaced from DLP stock
1. Item details:
    In Sl. No: C1517082018 (healthy)
    Out Sl. No: CH1516121001 (faulty)
2. Material to be recouped / repaired at the earliest.
3. Investigation report to be submitted for closure of the NCR.

In TS#13 TC2-VAC1 emergency inverter fault was reported by Metro Railway during their schedule checking at depot. MR has confirmed that particular VAC is not running in emergency mode.
On investigation it was found that EMI is physically not running. Emergency inverter was checked for any wire looseness or wrong connection, but no abnormalities were found. Further, it was checked at inverter terminal, following observations were made: 
a)	Pin no- 1 & 2 (110 V DC) found ok.
b)	Pin no- 3 (110 V DC) EMI ok signal found ok.
c)	Pin no- 4 (110 V DC) EMI ok signal found intermittent.
d)	Pin no- 5&6 (110 V DC) START + & START- found ok.
e)	Pin no 7,8 & 9 (U, V & W) output voltage is inconsistent.
Based on same inverter is declared faulty by FTI. After replacement function test done & found ok.
Correction/ corrective action result: 
1.	The faulty inverter is replaced with a healthy emergency inverter and VAC function checked and it is working normally. 
    In (healthy) Sl. No: 0204 (modified)	 
    Out (faulty) Sl. No: 0521 (modified)
		
2.	Investigation report to be submitted for closer of the NCR. 
Note: – This is 68th modified emergency inverter failure.

     stock on 23.11.2025 and problem resolved.

During rake (TS#15) offering to mainline on 11.11.2025 the following faults observed in MC1 bogie 2 when DBV applied in mode selector OFF position by Motorman. 
1.	FDS release DCL failure B2
2.	CDS DCL deviation B2

Defect was communicated via a job card by customer. During investigation at IBL03 we found major air leakage from EBCU KR-6AA relay valve port of MC1 BG1 BCU1 & MC2 BG2 BCU2 under braking and motoring conditions.
To rectify the above fault OEM decided to replace the faulty KR-6AA relay valve of MC1 BG1 BCU1 from DLP stock and TS#15 MC2 BG2 BCU2 faulty KR-6AA relay valve was swapped from healthy KR-6AA relay valve of TS#17 MC2 BG2 BCU2 to make train healthy for mainline revenue services.
KBI confirmed that the KR-6AA relay valve at both the location mentioned above was faulty on 20.11.2025 after investigation.

1.	TS#15 MC1 BG1 BCU1 KR-6AA relay valve details:
Faulty serial no:2923276163                 Healthy serial  no:2920222365(DLP)
TS#15 MC2 BG2 BCU2 KR-6AA relay valve details:
Faulty serial no:2923196094                 Healthy serial  no:2920042211(TS#17)
2.	M/s KBI has to submit the failure investigation report.

During routine inspection of electrical panels, observed cables are loose at contact (1L1) of ILDCC2 contactor of TC1 for Train set 16. We tried to properly tighten the cables but failed. After further investigation, it was observed that locking screw is not getting any feed after applying torque, freely rotating. It may happen due to improper thread inside the hole. To make train healthy contactor being replaced from DLP stock.   
Correction/ corrective action result:
1. Faulty relay being replaced with healthy relay which was taken from DLP stock to continue the operation.
2. M/s AAL has to provide 1 no. Interior light DC contactor 2 (ILDCC2) immediately to maintain the DLP stock at Depot.

During the schedule maintenance of MR607, Metro Railway has reported that for DMC1, VAC1, RCCB (QF01) is tripping immediately whenever VAC was made to run in normal mode.
When checked on Mona PTU, it is found that RCCB trip fault is active in event log. Further when phisically checked in VAC control panel, RCCB (QF01) is found in tripped condition. For investigation purpose, RCCB was reset and all sub component of VACs are made to run one by one in service mode through MONA PTU. When Compressor 2 was triggered, immediately RCCB again got tripped.
The reffered compressor was checked for winding resistance accross all the phases & found balanced i.e., 1.2 Ω, 1.3 Ω & 1.3 Ω. Further all related wirings are checked for any looseness, cut mark or damage, found everything normal.
When compressor winding is checked with respect to ground. It is found that for compressor 2 winding (U, V & W) Vs ground is showing value measured as 4 Ω for all the 3 phases which should actually show as open loop. 
Based on above, compressor 2 fault has been assured. VAC SI. NO. G18-4082.
Faulty si. no. - 18BG9789Z
Healthy si. no. - 24DC3413Z
Correction/ corrective action result:
1. Item to be repaired/ replaced in DLP at the earliest.
2. Investigation report to be submitted for closure of the NCR.
Note: This is 11th compressor failure after flashing of SW 0.12.

MR has reported for the Smoke Detected fault in mainline during revenue operation.
Previously, same fault appeared twice in mainline on 11.12.25 & 12.12.25.For the 1st time fault when checked in depot, found some foreign particles near to smoke sensor. thus, smoke sensor has been cleaned & reinstalled to VAC. for the 2nd time when checked at depot, no abnormalities observed. Thus, kept under monitoring. In both the cases, fault was not active in depot during investigation.
Again during 3rd time failure also, fault was not active in depot during investigation. All related wiring from equipment (smoke sensor) side to VAC control panel has been checked and found ok. Health of KA2 relay has also been checked by simulating smoke in depot and found ok.
Additionally, smoke sensor has also been swapped with the other VAC of same train, but fault not popped up. 
Since every day during revenue operation, reffered fault is getting reported thus on suspect basis this time smoke sensor, of TC1, VAC1 has been replaced with the DLP stock and is kept under monitoring.
Healthy SI. NO.: C1516091008
Faulty SI. NO.: CH1516091024.
Correction/ Corrective action result:
1. Smoke sensor has been replaced from DLP stock.
Items details: 
Healthy SI. NO.: C1516091008
Faulty SI. NO.: CH1516091024.
2. Material to be recouped/ repaired at the earliest.
3. Investigation report to be submitted for closure of the NCR.

MR has reported for the Smoke detected fault in mainline during revenue operation.
This is the 4th time when same fault appeared in mainline for the same location. It may please be noted that just on 23.12.25, smoke sensor has been replaced from DLP stock on suspect basis as no abnormalities were observed from wiring side during investigation.
This time fault was active in depot during investigation. All related wiring from equipement (smoke sensor) side to VAC control panel has been checked and found ok. Firstly, on suspect basis PLC has been replaced with DLP stock but fault not got reset. Then once again Smoke Sensor has been replaced with another smoke sensor from DLP stock and fault got reset.
The faulty smoke sensor failed with in 24 hrs. of opeartion, thus raising a question mark on the reliability of the Equipment.

1. Smoke sensor has been replaced from DLP stock.

Healthy SI. NO.: C1515091015
Faulty SI. NO.: CH1516091008.

TS#15-DMC1-R1 -Saloon door - Left side spindle rod broken at Bengal chemical platform in East bond and door was partially open condition after door close command given by Motorman at 10.57 am during revenue operation on 27/12/2025.
The defective door couldn't be manually closed by the Motorman. Train departed from Bengal chemical at 11.10 am with two maintenance staff guarding the door and arrived at Sector V platform at 11.19 am instead of 11.06 am.
KBI jointly checked broken spindle with BEML and Metro Railway and confirmed the spindle rod broken at end of mounting bracket at CP Depot.

1.	M/s KBI has replaced faulty Spindle rod from DLP Stock & Serial no is not available in healthy and faulty Spindle rod.
2.	Saloon door function checked after replacement and found no any Vibration and shaking from Spindle rod.
3.	Pull force 65 N and other installation parameters found within range.
4.	Please recouped the material With FIR.
5.	Photos attached in Annexure

TS#16-DMC2-R1 -Saloon door - abnormal sound reported by Metro Railway in Saloon door operation during revenue operation on 30/12/2025.
We have attended the abnormal sound issue in the saloon door at the Depot after revenue operation on 30/12/2025 and It has been noticed that the Left side spindle rod was broken.
KBI Team ( Local and H.O executives) jointly checked broken spindle with BEML and Metro Railway on 02/01/2026 and confirmed the spindle rod broken at end of mounting bracket at CP Depot.

1.	Spindle rod was not available in DLP stock, Hence KBI requested BEML to take spindle on loan basis from Metro railway. BEML taken 1 no spindle-LHS on loan basis from customer as KBI confirmed to return the spindle to Customer.   
2.	M/s KBI has replaced faulty Spindle rod from GA Stock (Loan basis)on 02/02/2026 & Serial no is not available in healthy and faulty Spindle rod.
3.	Saloon door function checked after replacement and found no any Vibration and shaking from Spindle rod.
4.	Pull force 70 N and other installation parameters found within range.
5.	Please recouped the material With FIR.
6.	Photos attached in Annexure

column5:
Vertical Damper Brackets - RHS
Vertical Damper Brackets - LHS
column6:
Vertical damper brackets are cracking in revenue trains since 2024 to till the date and TS#01 to 14 - All Vertical damper brackets replacement done along with fire barrier assembly in Motor cars only on January 2025 and brackets did not replace in Trailer Cars (TC Cars).
164 nos. New Vertical damper brackets again started failing from July 2025 to till the date after Vertical dampers brackets replacement.
Our R&D Team proposed New design Vertical damper brackets & same approved by GC/KMRCL and installed new design brackets in 2 Train sets (TS#03 & 15) in Dec 2025 at CP Depot Kolkata and kept under monitoring for 4 months.
V.D.Brackets are continues cracking other Train sets on regular basis except TS#03 & 15. 
TS#01 to 17 – Failed Vertical Damper brackets As Per Annexure-1/CPD/889
(1)TS#01 to 17 – Vertical Damper Brackets failed = LHS -80 NOS and RHS 84 NOS.
(2)Spare Vertical Damper Brackets are required (TS#01to 17) for DLP = 10 NOS LHS and 10 RHS.
Corrective Action : 
Last 15 days Vertical damper brackets are cracking in revenue train sets on daily basis and We are replacing with old brackets ,that stock are also exhausting, however We are required new design / old design Vertical damper brackets on urgent basis to make train healthy (Train sets#01 to 17) otherwise Train sets cannot send to revenue operation in Crack vertical damper brackets condition and Customer will keep the Train sets in BEML account due to above issue. 
Total Vertical Damper Brackets are required with Spares = 90 nos. LHS and 94 nos. RHS

TS#17 DMC2 DL1 saloon door was found not closing during close command given through left door close push button. 
Further, TCMS VDU fault record showed "DCU fault broken wire in the circuit of door drive motor DL1" in car no 1217 (DMC2).
During investigation it was observed by KBI that TS#17 DMC2 L1 saloon door DCU not functioning efficiently. 
KBI field officer confirmed that above mentioned saloon door DCU is faulty after detailed investigation.
1. M/s KBI has replaced faulty DCU (Sr. no: 315281-00000812-1023) from DLP stock (healthy DCU Si. No. 315281-00000155-0917) on 29.01.2026.
2. Saloon door function checked after replacement of saloon door DCU and found DMC2 DL1 door working normally. 
3. Please submit FIR for NCR closer.
